tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary focuses on scalar i1 tests and commutativity variations, but does not mention vector type coverage. The patch supports isIntOrIntVectorTy(1) but summary's test coverage section only discusses scalar operand permutations.

alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define <2 x i1> @xor_logic_and_logic_or_vector(<2 x i1> %c, <2 x i1> %x, <2 x i1> %y) {
  %1 = xor <2 x i1> %x, splat (i1 true)
  %r = select <2 x i1> %c, <2 x i1> %1, <2 x i1> %y
  ret <2 x i1> %r
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define <2 x i1> @xor_logic_and_logic_or_vector(<2 x i1> %c, <2 x i1> %x, <2 x i1> %y) {
#0:
  %a = and <2 x i1> %c, %x
  %o = or <2 x i1> %c, %y
  %r = xor <2 x i1> %a, %o
  ret <2 x i1> %r
}
=>
define <2 x i1> @xor_logic_and_logic_or_vector(<2 x i1> %c, <2 x i1> %x, <2 x i1> %y) {
#0:
  %#1 = xor <2 x i1> %x, { 1, 1 }
  %r = select <2 x i1> %c, <2 x i1> %#1, <2 x i1> %y
  ret <2 x i1> %r
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

tests.outside_scope_reason: All known tests in summary use select instructions to represent logical and/or operations. This test uses direct and/or binary operators on i1, which matches m_LogicalAnd/m_LogicalOr but isn't covered in the summary's test examples.

alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i1 @xor_logic_and_logic_or_direct_ops(i1 %c, i1 %x, i1 %y) {
  %1 = xor i1 %x, true
  %r = select i1 %c, i1 %1, i1 %y
  ret i1 %r
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @xor_logic_and_logic_or_direct_ops(i1 %c, i1 %x, i1 %y) {
#0:
  %a = and i1 %c, %x
  %o = or i1 %c, %y
  %r = xor i1 %a, %o
  ret i1 %r
}
=>
define i1 @xor_logic_and_logic_or_direct_ops(i1 %c, i1 %x, i1 %y) {
#0:
  %#1 = xor i1 %x, 1
  %r = select i1 %c, i1 %#1, i1 %y
  ret i1 %r
}

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary discusses variable operand permutations but doesn't cover cases where one of the non-common operands (B or C) is a constant value. The pattern still matches but constant folding interaction is outside stated scope.

  ret i1 %r
}
